description_long: This object consists of four industrial metal barrels, each with a weathered and worn appearance, arranged in a line. The barrels are painted in distinct colors: blue, red, yellow, and gray, with visible signs of rust, scratches, and grime, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. Each barrel features two prominent horizontal reinforcement rings and slightly indented sides, typical of oil or chemical drums. The tops of the barrels are closed, with no visible openings or lids, and some barrels display faded markings or stains. The surfaces are mostly matte with patches of glossiness where the paint is less worn. These barrels are commonly found in urban, industrial, or construction environments, often used for storage or waste collection.
description: A set of four weathered metal barrels in blue, red, yellow, and gray, each with reinforced rings and visible signs of rust and wear. The barrels are closed and show typical industrial use.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four barrels aligned in a row, with the tops visible and the colors stacked from front to back: gray, yellow, red, and blue.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full height and cylindrical shape of each barrel, with all four colors clearly visible and the reinforcement rings prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view with the tops of the barrels visible, showing the blue barrel in front and the others behind, highlighting the circular tops and weathered paint.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing all four barrels, with the blue barrel now on the left and the gray on the right, emphasizing the color sequence and surface wear.
